title: Cisco Troubleshooting Capstone Lab
description: A capstone troubleshooting lab covering distinct VLAN, inter-VLAN routing, OSPF, ACL, trunk, and interface scenarios. Diagnose each issue with show commands and apply the correct fix.

tasks.prompt: Scenario 1 — Users in VLAN 30 cannot reach the network. Inspect VLAN assignments on SW-ACCESS to confirm VLAN 30 exists.
tasks.explanation: Start VLAN troubleshooting by verifying the VLAN database and which ports are assigned to each VLAN.

tasks.prompt: Scenario 1 — Port Fa0/12 is in VLAN 1 but should serve VLAN 30 (Marketing). Assign the correct access VLAN.
tasks.explanation: An access port in the wrong VLAN isolates the host from its intended subnet and default gateway.

tasks.prompt: Scenario 2 — A user reports no link on desk port Gi1/0/5. Check interface status to see if the port is err-disabled or down.
tasks.explanation: Interface status shows admin/oper state, VLAN, speed, and type—quickly revealing shut down or err-disabled ports.

tasks.prompt: Scenario 2 — Gi1/0/5 is administratively down. Bring the interface up.
tasks.explanation: A port left in shutdown state after maintenance blocks all traffic even when the cable is connected.

tasks.prompt: Scenario 3 — Inter-VLAN routing fails between VLAN 10 and VLAN 20. Check L3 interface status on DSW-CORE.
tasks.explanation: This command reveals whether SVIs are up/up and have correct IP addresses assigned.

tasks.prompt: Scenario 3 — DSW-CORE is not routing between VLANs. Enable Layer 3 routing on the multilayer switch.
tasks.explanation: Without 'ip routing', a multilayer switch acts as a Layer 2 device and cannot forward between SVIs.

tasks.prompt: Scenario 4 — VLAN 20 hosts have no gateway. Enter configuration for the missing VLAN 20 SVI.
tasks.explanation: Each routed VLAN requires an SVI. Missing SVIs are a common cause of inter-VLAN connectivity failures.

tasks.prompt: Scenario 4 — Assign gateway address 192.168.20.1/24 to the VLAN 20 SVI.
tasks.explanation: Hosts in VLAN 20 use this address as their default gateway to reach other subnets.

tasks.prompt: Scenario 5 — Branch office cannot reach HQ subnet 10.50.0.0/16. Inspect the routing table on R1.
tasks.explanation: The routing table shows whether the router has a path to the destination network via connected, static, or dynamic routes.

tasks.prompt: Scenario 5 — Add a static route to 10.50.0.0/16 via next-hop 203.0.113.1 on R1.
tasks.explanation: A missing static route prevents the router from forwarding packets toward remote networks not learned dynamically.

tasks.prompt: Scenario 6 — OSPF neighbors are not forming on R2. Verify OSPF adjacency state.
tasks.explanation: An empty neighbor table indicates mismatched area IDs, ACL blocks, passive interfaces, or network statement errors.

tasks.prompt: Scenario 6 — R2's Gi0/0 (10.1.1.2/30) is not in OSPF area 0. Advertise 10.1.1.0/30 into area 0.
tasks.explanation: Interfaces not covered by a network statement do not participate in OSPF and cannot form adjacencies.

tasks.prompt: Scenario 7 — Management host 192.168.99.10 cannot SSH to R3. Check ACL hit counters to find the blocking rule.
tasks.explanation: ACL hit counters reveal which permit or deny statement is matching traffic, pinpointing overly restrictive rules.

tasks.prompt: Scenario 7 — ACL 10 is missing a permit for the management subnet. Permit 192.168.99.0/24 in standard ACL 10.
tasks.explanation: Standard ACLs filter on source address. A missing permit before the implicit deny blocks management traffic.

tasks.prompt: Scenario 8 — ACL 10 exists but is not filtering traffic on R3. Apply ACL 10 inbound on GigabitEthernet0/0.
tasks.explanation: An ACL has no effect until bound to an interface. Verify both the ACL content and its interface application.

tasks.prompt: Scenario 9 — VLAN 40 traffic is not reaching the core. Inspect trunk links on SW-DIST to verify allowed VLANs.
tasks.explanation: Trunk status shows encapsulation, native VLAN, and which VLANs are allowed and actively forwarding.

tasks.prompt: Scenario 9 — VLAN 40 is pruned from the trunk to the core. Add VLAN 40 to the allowed list on Gi0/1.
tasks.explanation: If a VLAN is not in the trunk allowed list, traffic for that VLAN cannot traverse the inter-switch link.

tasks.prompt: Scenario 10 — The uplink on SW-ACCESS Fa0/24 is stuck in access mode. Configure it as an 802.1Q trunk.
tasks.explanation: An uplink left in access mode carries only one VLAN, breaking connectivity for all other VLANs on remote switches.

tasks.prompt: Scenario 11 — CRC errors on R1 Gi0/1 suggest a duplex mismatch with the ISP router. Set full duplex.
tasks.explanation: A duplex mismatch causes collisions, CRC errors, and intermittent packet loss on the link.

tasks.prompt: Scenario 12 — Final verification: review the full running configuration of R1's GigabitEthernet0/0 after all fixes.
tasks.explanation: Reviewing the interface running config confirms IP address, OSPF settings, ACL bindings, and speed/duplex in one place.
